title: Sorry Your Insurance Doesnt Cover Evas
display_title: Sorry Your Insurance Doesnt Cover Evas
wikitext:
[]: Sorry, Your Insurance Doesn't Cover Evas             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Wed Sep 09, 2009 1:56 pm What would you do if your house and possessions got totally destroyed in a battle and your insurance company couldn't cover your losses and you were forced to live in a shelter home? I would sneak up to the Eva that destroyed my house after every battle, and key the hard parts of its "shoe". A better alternative to jumping the pilots, which would earn me an ass whipping by Section 2 goons.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Wed Sep 09, 2009 2:02 pm I always assumed living in tokyo 3 was like living in alaska their was some kind of government incentive for putting up with the bad stuff. Alaskans get a money maybe tokyo 3 people get one or some big tax breaks or hell in a world post 2nd impact maybe its the only place to get good jobs for some people.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Wed Sep 09, 2009 2:35 pm I think that Misato tried to prevent such massive damages when she could (especially after having to respond to so many complaints during episode 9). But I'm sure Nerv has a crack legal team that can strike down any lawsuit filed against it as a result of damages, so that option is out of the question. I would just try to rebuild, but having an Angel attack every other week would eventualy force me to move somewhere safer, like Compton in LA.

[]: Re: Sorry, Your Insurance Doesn't Cover Evas             Quote Post by Synapsid » Wed Sep 09, 2009 2:38 pm Sammaeloo wrote: What would you do if your house and possessions got totally destroyed in a battle and your insurance company couldn't cover your losses and you were forced to live in a shelter home? I would sneak up to the Eva that destroyed my house after every battle, and key the hard parts of its "shoe". A better alternative to jumping around the pilots, which would earn me an ass whipping by Section 2 goons.

[]: Re: Sorry, Your Insurance Doesn't Cover Evas             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Sep 09, 2009 2:47 pm Sammaeloo wrote: What would you do if your house and possessions got totally destroyed in a battle and your insurance company couldn't cover your losses and you were forced to live in a shelter home?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Sep 09, 2009 2:49 pm I don't know what I would do, but I would be mega pissed if a shell casing landed on my car/house.

[]: Re: Sorry, Your Insurance Doesn't Cover Evas             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Wed Sep 09, 2009 3:52 pm Synapsid wrote: Sammaeloo wrote: What would you do if your house and possessions got totally destroyed in a battle and your insurance company couldn't cover your losses and you were forced to live in a shelter home? I would sneak up to the Eva that destroyed my house after every battle, and key the hard parts of its "shoe". A better alternative to jumping around the pilots, which would earn me an ass whipping by Section 2 goons.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Sep 09, 2009 3:55 pm I wouldn't live in Tokyo-3 unless I was on-staff with nerv in the first place (everyone else with anything approaching common sense got the hell out of there after Sachiel), but assuming that fact I'm sure they take care of their own. At the very least you know there are plenty of vacant accommodations you can move to, and mass transit systems mean you probably don't need a car anyway. Besides, even if you were covered by the time they got around to dealing with your insurance claim the world we be over anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Wed Sep 09, 2009 4:47 pm whatcha mean, insurance won't cover it? NERV should have a chunk of their huuuuuuge budget especially dedicated to such things. i mean wtf, i'm risking my life living in angel central and i don't get any indemnisation? screw you. also, they should have had not only buildings that can go underground, but platforms that allowed people to park their cars and then took them underground, as well. i'd be right pissed if my car got stomped on by an angel or an eva. in all honesty, i would live outside the city and commute every day. better having to wake up earlier than normal every day, than having to look for a new house/apartment four times a year.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Sep 09, 2009 5:00 pm Nerv: "If you have a problem with the way we do things then we could always not save all of your lives. Yeah? How does that sound? Psh, Pedestrians."

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Wed Sep 09, 2009 5:05 pm If you try to sue Gendo has you removed by section 2 for disrupting the scenario. He does this at least three times a week that is why Seele is pissed at him they needed to bye more land under the turnpike to dispose of people.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Sep 09, 2009 5:54 pm They could have saved a bunch of money by switching to Geico.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed Sep 09, 2009 6:09 pm If I lived in Tokyo 3, I’d put most of my stuff in storage somewhere out side the city and then just rent an apartment. Then when it inevitably gets smashed, it’s the landlord’s problem and not mine.

[]:             Quote Post by Cypher » Thu Sep 10, 2009 3:08 am After the 2nd, 3rd or even 4th time my home was demolished I'd probably just live in a tent on the outskirts of town. That way it won't matter if lose UN ordinance, giant feet or cross shaped explosions destroy my home. Even if Sahaquiel lands were I was camping its only another $50 to replace the tent and I get a free crater to squat in.

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Sat Sep 12, 2009 7:45 pm Cypher wrote: Even if Sahaquiel lands were I was camping its only another $50 to replace the tent and I get a free crater to squat in.

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Sat Sep 12, 2009 7:48 pm Move the hell out of Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Sun Sep 13, 2009 6:37 am Sneak in, end set up camp inside one of the Magi...there's enough room, and I'm sure no one will notice. I'll just have to sneak out for supplies every now and again

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Mon Sep 14, 2009 12:58 am DatDude wrote: alaska their was some kind of government incentive for putting up with the bad stuff. Alaskans get a money.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Sep 14, 2009 4:26 am Legendary wrote: ...we've got SO MUCH OIL WE STILL HAVE ENOUGH TO GIVE EVERY ALASKAN A BATHTUB FULL, the government feels obligated to give us some proceeds for LIVING IN AWESOME LAND.

[]:             Quote Post by ReiearthBlue » Fri Sep 18, 2009 2:54 pm I would think that getting your house/car stomped on by angel would be considered an act of God. and thus covered by insurance.
